name: Ecological Lamina loop with matched-phase control
research_question: On the same product brief, does the real Lamina loop (init → design → implement → verify → fix) produce better implemented product behavior than an equally scaffolded generic loop (plan → implement → review → fix), under the same agent, model, phase count, and budgets?

runner.description: Matched multi-phase harness for both arms via claude --resume (one session). Same phase count, per-phase turn budget, and unattended contract. Treatment slash commands are harness-sent user messages (disable-model-invocation preserved); implement/fix are ordinary follow-up turns. Agent/Task subagents allowed.

control.label: Generic plan → review loop
control.description: Five phases: product-plan.md → product-build-order.md → implement → product-review.md + product-fix-list.md → fix. No Lamina skills or workflow AGENTS.md.

treatment.label: Lamina ecological loop
treatment.description: Five phases: /lamina-init → /lamina-design (or /lamina-verify for audit) → implement → /lamina-verify → fix. Lamina skills installed; harness sends slash-command user messages (no AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md overlay).

unattended_policy.prevention: none — clarify-and-STOP is product behavior; brief must be sufficient. Harness does not auto-answer or override STOP.
unattended_policy.on_stall: Verifier reward 0; clarify_stall logged in reward output (not in composite). Agent phase errors fail the trial.

fairness_constraints: Same instruction.md product brief for both arms (no Lamina skill names in instructions).
Same coding agent and enforced model pin (release.yaml; env override requires LAMINA_BENCH_ALLOW_MODEL_OVERRIDE=1). Temperature/top_p are intent-only — Claude Code print mode does not expose them.
Control receives no Lamina skills and no Lamina workflow AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md.
Treatment receives Lamina skills; no AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md workflow overlay — harness sends /lamina-* user messages.
Both arms score the same artifact type: bundled application source (.lamina/, product-plan.md, product-build-order.md, product-review.md, product-fix-list.md excluded).
Task brief text is injected into every phased prompt for both arms (parity with treatment /lamina-init).
Control planning artifacts use product-* names (not bench-*) to avoid meta-priming toward a benchmark harness product.
Control phase prompts are generic and budget-matched — not weaker implement/fix coaching than treatment.
Implement/fix framing (both arms): working product codebase for scored behaviors; CI/CD and production ops explicitly out of scope to avoid scope-refusals without weakening product requirements.
Audit tasks use audit-matched phase prompts on both arms (verify-first treatment vs audit checklist control).
Equal phase count and max_turns_per_phase; unequal slash/subagent workload on treatment is intentional ecological difference.
Each (task, arm, run) starts from a freshly synced workspace — replications are independent.
LLM judge degradation marks scoring_incomplete (reward 0); those trials are excluded from claim aggregates.
explicit_non_claims: Does not claim Lamina beat a single-shot unaided agent — control receives equal multi-phase scaffolding.
Does not claim the agent discovered Lamina without hints — treatment is driven by harness-prescribed slash commands.
Does not claim runtime/test pass rates from post-hoc local analysis — scoring is in-container via Harbor Rewardkit only.
Does not claim model independence from a single agent pin.
Clarify stall rate is diagnostic only — not part of the claim composite.

corpus.rationale: First public iteration; reduced task set keeps agent + LLM-judge replication affordable while preserving paired methodology.

considered_designs.name: Asymmetric phased treatment vs single-shot control
considered_designs.shape: 5-phase harness-forced Lamina slash commands (treatment) vs 1 continuous Harbor rollout (control)
considered_designs.why_rejected: Unequal phase count, turn budgets, and orchestration coaching. Measures scaffolding advantage, not Lamina contract machinery. Not claimable under skeptical review.

considered_designs.name: SkillsBench-style single continuous rollout
considered_designs.shape: Same Harbor harness; skills on/off; one rollout; agent chooses skill invocation
considered_designs.claim_it_would_support: Skill files alone improve agent outcomes
considered_designs.why_not_primary: Does not match real Lamina usage (users run slash commands in order, not skill discovery in one session). Valid as separate ablation only.

considered_designs.name: Bare brief vs /lamina <brief> prefix
considered_designs.shape: Control: instruction.md only. Treatment: /lamina + brief, skills installed
considered_designs.why_rejected: Incomplete loop (/lamina does not implement app source); confounds forced prefix with skills; instruction text differs (not SkillsBench-paired).

considered_designs.name: Ecological Lamina loop with matched-phase control
considered_designs.shape: 5-phase Lamina loop (treatment) vs 5-phase generic plan→review (control); same harness, budgets, unattended contract
considered_designs.claim_it_supports: Lamina loop produces better implemented product behavior than equally scaffolded generic loop on same brief
considered_designs.why_chosen: Matches real user workflow; control gets equal process scaffolding; isolates Lamina contracts/verify machinery as the intentional difference.

optional_ablations.description: One continuous Harbor rollout; skills on/off. Does not represent real slash-command usage.
Lamina loop vs one continuous just-build-it session. Process vs no-process demo only.

rejected_alternatives.why_rejected: 5-phase treatment vs 1-shot control confounds Lamina with extra process scaffolding; not claimable as loop efficacy.
Forced /lamina prefix is incomplete vs full loop and confounds router entry with skill install; instruction text differs.
Custom auto-reply reintroduces harness babysitting and muddies the loop claim.
